questions.q: In which Indian state is the Kamakhya Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Kamakhya Temple stands on Nilachal Hill in Assam, near the city of Guwahati.

questions.q: Near which major city is the Kamakhya Temple situated?
questions.explanation: The temple is located on Nilachal Hill in Guwahati, the largest city of Assam.

questions.q: To which deity is the Kamakhya Temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to the goddess Kamakhya, a form of the Mother Goddess (Shakti).

questions.q: The Kamakhya Temple is one of the most revered shrines belonging to which group of sacred sites?
questions.explanation: Kamakhya is counted among the most important Shakti Peethas, sites associated with the goddess Sati.

questions.q: On which hill does the Kamakhya Temple stand?
questions.explanation: The temple is located atop Nilachal Hill (also called Nilshaila) overlooking Guwahati.

questions.q: Which famous annual festival, celebrating the goddess's menstruation, is held at Kamakhya?
questions.explanation: The Ambubachi Mela, marking the goddess's annual menstruation cycle, draws huge crowds of devotees.

questions.q: Which broad form of Hindu worship is centred on the goddess at Kamakhya?
questions.explanation: Kamakhya is a major centre of Shaktism and Tantric worship of the Divine Feminine.

questions.q: What kind of object is enshrined and worshipped in the temple's sanctum, rather than a typical idol?
options: A natural rock cleft (yoni) fed by a spring
questions.explanation: Instead of an image, a natural yoni-shaped rock cleft, kept moist by an underground spring, is worshipped.

questions.q: The river flowing near Guwahati below Nilachal Hill is the:
questions.explanation: Nilachal Hill rises above the Brahmaputra River, which flows through Guwahati.

questions.q: Kamakhya is primarily worshipped as a form of which goddess?
questions.explanation: Kamakhya is venerated as a powerful form of the Mother Goddess, Shakti or Devi.

questions.q: In Shakti Peetha legend, which part of the goddess Sati's body is said to have fallen at Kamakhya?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Sati's yoni fell here, which is why the goddess is worshipped in yoni form.

questions.q: The current main temple structure is largely attributed to the rebuilding efforts of which 16th-century kingdom?
questions.explanation: The temple was rebuilt in the 16th century under the Koch kings, notably associated with Naranarayan.

questions.q: Which Koch king is traditionally credited with the major 16th-century reconstruction of the Kamakhya Temple?
questions.explanation: The Koch king Naranarayan, with his brother Chilarai, is credited with rebuilding the temple around 1565.

questions.q: The distinctive beehive-shaped dome (shikhara) of the Kamakhya Temple is characteristic of which regional style?
questions.explanation: The temple's hemispherical beehive shikhara is a hallmark of the distinctive Assamese Nilachal architectural style.

questions.q: During the Ambubachi Mela, the temple's sanctum is typically:
options: Opened for a special grand abhishekam
Closed for several days, then reopened
Decorated with thousands of lamps only
questions.explanation: The sanctum is closed for about three days during the goddess's symbolic menstruation, then ceremonially reopened.

questions.q: The Kamakhya complex is associated with a group of goddess temples often described as the:
questions.explanation: Kamakhya is linked with the ten Mahavidyas, with several of these goddesses having shrines on Nilachal Hill.

questions.q: Which medieval invasion is traditionally said to have damaged or destroyed an earlier temple at the site before its 16th-century rebuilding?
options: A campaign associated with Kalapahar/Bengal Sultanate forces
The sack by Mahmud of Ghazni
questions.explanation: An earlier structure is said to have been destroyed in the medieval period, commonly attributed in tradition to Kalapahar of the Bengal Sultanate.

questions.q: The Kamakhya Temple is known for the ritual practice of what kind of offering, common in Shakta tradition?
options: Floating offerings down a river only
questions.explanation: As a major Shakta shrine, Kamakhya is well known for the practice of animal sacrifice as an offering to the goddess.

questions.q: The inner sanctum (garbhagriha) of Kamakhya is best described as:
options: A tall open hall flooded with light
A dark cave-like chamber reached by descending steps
questions.explanation: Devotees descend into a dark, cave-like chamber where the yoni rock and spring are located.

questions.q: Which of these statements about Kamakhya's UNESCO status is correct?
options: It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site
It is not a UNESCO World Heritage Site
It was delisted by UNESCO in 2010
It is a UNESCO Natural Heritage Site
questions.explanation: The Kamakhya Temple is a major pilgrimage site but is not inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: The name 'Kamakhya' is most closely connected with which concept in tradition?
options: Desire/love (Kama) and the goddess granting wishes
questions.explanation: The name is linked to Kama (desire/love); Kamakhya is revered as the goddess of desire who fulfils wishes.

questions.q: The Kamakhya Temple is one of the principal religious centres of which historical region of Assam?
questions.explanation: The site lies in the ancient region of Kamarupa (Pragjyotisha), long renowned as a centre of goddess worship and Tantra.

questions.q: Approximately when was the major surviving reconstruction of the Kamakhya Temple completed under the Koch kings?
questions.explanation: The Koch reconstruction associated with Naranarayan is generally dated to around 1565 CE.

questions.q: The Kamakhya Temple's plan combines a tall sanctum with a series of chambers; the large domed sanctum sits over which structural feature?
options: A cruciform base with a beehive shikhara above the garbhagriha
A stepped pyramid like a ziggurat
questions.explanation: The temple has a cruciform base supporting the hemispherical beehive shikhara over the sanctum, a defining Nilachal feature.

questions.q: Which Koch general, brother of king Naranarayan, is associated with patronage of the temple's reconstruction?
questions.explanation: Chilarai (Shukladhwaj), the celebrated general and brother of Naranarayan, is linked to the temple's 16th-century rebuilding.

questions.q: Besides Kamakhya herself, Nilachal Hill hosts shrines to several Mahavidyas. Which of the following is among them?
questions.explanation: The goddess Tara is one of the Mahavidyas with a shrine on Nilachal Hill alongside Kamakhya.

questions.q: A medieval Tantric text strongly associated with Kamakhya and Kamarupa goddess worship is the:
questions.explanation: The Kalika Purana, composed in the Kamarupa region, glorifies the worship of Kamakhya and details her rites.

questions.q: During the Ambubachi period, the natural spring at the sanctum is traditionally said to turn what colour, symbolising the goddess's menstruation?
questions.explanation: The spring water is traditionally believed to take on a reddish tinge during Ambubachi, symbolising menstruation.

questions.q: Which dynasty that ruled medieval Assam later extended significant patronage to the Kamakhya Temple after the Koch period?
questions.explanation: The Ahom rulers of Assam became important later patrons of the Kamakhya Temple following the Koch reconstruction.

questions.q: The principal building material of the rebuilt Kamakhya Temple's superstructure is best described as:
options: Stone with brick and a plastered/mortar finish
questions.explanation: The temple is built largely of stone and brick with a mortar/plaster finish, characteristic of the Nilachal style.
